key_insight: A fully variational CutFEM formulation for finite-strain elasticity can be built directly from an energy functional, enabling automatic differentiation of stresses and tangents while preserving cut-independent stability.
engineering_relevance: The method offers a robust unfitted simulation route for complex geometries and moving boundaries, with optimal convergence and stable conditioning for nonlinear hyperelastic problems.
method: Augment the hyperelastic bulk energy with Nitsche boundary terms and ghost-penalty stabilization.
Derive the residual and symmetrized tangent by successive variations of the energy functional.
Use automatic differentiation to generate first Piola--Kirchhoff stress and elasticity tensors for different material models.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports optimal h-convergence for linear, quadratic, and cubic elements, plus cut-independent coercivity, continuity, and an O(h^-2) condition number bound, including corner-singularity analysis and local mesh refinement tests.
watch_item: Accuracy can still be limited at mixed Dirichlet--Neumann corner singularities unless local mesh refinement is applied.
paper_title: A Unified CutFEM Formulation for Finite-Strain Elasticity: Energy Minimisation and Corner Singularities
